title: Bridge Essay: Functional MRI findings in personality disorders: A review.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Wind/Pride** (Phase 4) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Wind/Pride to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Functional MRI findings in personality disorders: Areview.** — J Neuroimaging. PMID: 34468063. DOI: 10.1111/jon.12924. Personality disorders (PDs) have aprevalence of approximately 10% in the United States, translating to over 30 million people affected in just one country. The true prevalence of these disorders may be even higher, as the paucity of objective diagnostic criteria could be leading to underdiagnosis. Because little is known about the underlying neuropathologies of these disorders, patients are diagnosed using subjective criteria and treated nonspecifically.
